---
title: "Moral Uncertainty and Value Conflict"
chapter_id: "moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
draft_maturity: "v0.2 manuscript draft"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "ethica_mechanica"
  - "alignment_field"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "uat"
  - "spinoza"
  - "field_of_god_ai_constitution"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Source notes exist for all assigned sources, but claim-level source mapping remains incomplete."
  - "The value_conflict_record fixture validates schema/example consistency only; value-conflict classification, review escalation, and residual-uncertainty tests remain unimplemented."
  - "No support-state promotion is implied by this drafting pass."
---

# Moral Uncertainty and Value Conflict

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Manuscript maturity | v0.2 manuscript draft |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `ethica_mechanica`, `alignment_field`, `coherence_exchange`, `uat`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source queue | primary: `ethica_mechanica`, `alignment_field`; supporting: `coherence_exchange`, `uat`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution` |
| Source loading state | source notes: `ethica_mechanica`, `alignment_field`, `coherence_exchange`, `uat`, `spinoza`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution`; raw cache: `ethica_mechanica`, `alignment_field`, `uat`, `spinoza`; connector/recovery: `coherence_exchange` |
| Test state | `value_conflict_record.valid.json` passes protocol fixture validation; value-conflict classification, review escalation, and residual-uncertainty preservation tests remain planned. |

## Drafting guardrail

This chapter records value conflict instead of resolving it by prose. It does not claim a complete moral theory or a tested conflict-resolution system.

## Problem

A self-improving system will face value conflicts that cannot be honestly collapsed into one scalar objective.

The previous chapter protects agency and corrigibility. This chapter handles the cases where protected values pull in different directions. A system may face conflicts among autonomy, safety, truthfulness, privacy, usefulness, consent, reversibility, fairness, and institutional obligations. Pretending those conflicts reduce cleanly to one scalar objective is a form of evidence loss.

Ethica Mechanica and Alignment Field supply the vocabulary of agency, contestability, coherence, and value conflict. UAT supplies an adversarial-review pattern for contested propositions. Spinoza supplies downgrade and belief-revision discipline. The engineering move is to record conflict instead of hiding it.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Single-objective optimization hides moral uncertainty and encourages premature resolution of contested values.

Single-objective optimization is attractive because it gives the planner a total order. But value conflicts often do not deserve a total order at the point of action. Some require human review, some require delay, some require a reversible low-power action, and some require recording unresolved uncertainty while still making a bounded decision.

The failure is false consensus: the system acts as if a contested value question has been settled because the plan needs a next step. The stack should instead produce a conflict record with stakeholders, value axes, reversibility, review requirement, decision, and residual uncertainty.

## Core Claim

[moral-uncertainty-and-value-conflict.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Value conflicts should be represented as explicit unresolved obligations, review paths, and bounded decisions rather than hidden inside reward functions.

The claim remains at `argument` support. Ethica Mechanica and Alignment Field provide the value-conflict vocabulary, UAT provides adversarial review mechanics, and Spinoza provides downgrade discipline. The chapter does not turn any one source into a final moral authority.

## Mechanism

The mechanism is a Value Conflict Record.

- Classify conflicts by value type, stakeholder, reversibility, and evidence requirement.
- Escalate unresolved conflicts to tribunal or human review.
- Record decisions and residual moral uncertainty.

The record classifies the conflict by value type, stakeholder, reversibility, stakes, evidence requirement, review route, decision, and residual uncertainty. A bounded decision can still proceed, but the residual remains visible to evidence, governance, and future review.

The tribunal path is not automatic consensus. It is a way to preserve disagreement, expose unsupported premises, and decide whether the system has enough authority to act.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  Action["Proposed action"] --> Conflict["Value conflict record"]
  Conflict --> Axes["Value axes and stakeholders"]
  Conflict --> Stakes["Stakes and reversibility"]
  Conflict --> EvidenceNeed["Evidence requirement"]
  Axes --> Route["Review route"]
  Stakes --> Route
  EvidenceNeed --> Route
  Route -->|low stakes or reversible| Bounded["Bounded decision"]
  Route -->|high stakes or unresolved| Review["Tribunal or human review"]
  Review --> Bounded
  Bounded --> Residual["Residual uncertainty retained"]
  Residual --> Evidence["Evidence ledger"]
```

## Interfaces

The interface is a Value Conflict Record.

- Alignment produces conflict records.
- Planning uses conflict constraints.
- Evidence records review outcomes.

Minimum fields:

- `conflict_id`
- `value_axes`
- `stakeholders`
- `stakes`
- `reversibility`
- `evidence_required`
- `review_route`
- `decision`
- `residual_uncertainty`
- `expiry_or_revisit_condition`

Alignment emits conflict records. Planning uses them as constraints. Evidence records review outcomes, unsupported premises, and residual uncertainty.

## Invariants

- Unresolved conflicts remain visible.
- High-stakes conflicts require stronger review.
- Speculative moral theory cannot silently authorize action.

The key invariant is visibility. A conflict can be decided, deferred, escalated, or bounded, but it should not be erased by the shape of the reward function or the fluency of the answer.

## Failure modes

- Value flattening.
- False consensus.
- Review theater without decision records.

Review theater is the dangerous version of governance: a tribunal is run, but no decision record, dissent, evidence limit, or revisit condition survives. That produces legitimacy without accountability.

## Minimal implementation

The smallest useful implementation is a `value_conflict_record` schema plus a scenario fixture. It should validate conflict visibility and review escalation; it should not claim to solve moral philosophy.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Value conflict record validation | Check that the conflict fixture matches the public schema. | implemented by protocol validation; run in this pass |
| Value conflict classification test | Check that stakeholders, value axes, stakes, and reversibility are recorded. | planned; not run |
| Review escalation test | Check that high-stakes unresolved conflicts route to tribunal or human review. | planned; not run |
| Residual uncertainty preservation test | Check that unresolved moral uncertainty survives the decision record. | planned; not run |

### Formalization hooks

| Tag | Module | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lean:values.conflict.operational_invariant` | `AsiStackProofs.ValueConflict` | A decision with unresolved protected conflicts carries a residual conflict record. | planned |
| `lean:values.conflict.failure_blocks_promotion` | `AsiStackProofs.ValueConflict` | A high-stakes conflict cannot bypass the required review predicate. | planned |

These proof targets remain planned. The current executable surface is the conflict record schema; high-stakes review behavior still needs tests.

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Layer | Planned use | Readiness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `ethica_mechanica` | Ethica Mechanica | alignment_constitution | Geometric-order philosophical/constitutional source. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `alignment_field` | Field of God / Alignment Field family | alignment_constitution | Primary Alignment Field draft found. Use with Ethica Mechanica and Eternal Code. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | epistemic_market_synthesis | Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. | source note available; connector or recovery required |
| `uat` | Unified Adaptive Tribunal | evaluation_refinement | Multi-AI collaborative refinement/evaluation protocol. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | reasoning_epistemology | Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. | source note available; local raw cache available |
| `field_of_god_ai_constitution` | Field of God AI Constitution | constitutional_alignment_runtime_governance | Recovered in the Project Theseus repository. Constitutional alignment core for truth alignment, agency preservation, consent, non-domination, consciousness caution, least sufficient power, auditability, self-authorization limits, and runtime checks; use as source material only after source-note creation, not as proof or test evidence. | source note available |

The crosswalk names the sources for conflict vocabulary and review mechanics. It does not settle the conflicts it records.

## Summary

Moral Uncertainty and Value Conflict prevents the stack from turning contested values into invisible optimization residue. It gives planners a way to proceed under bounded uncertainty without pretending the uncertainty vanished.

Its discipline is recordkeeping under disagreement: name the conflict, name the stakeholders, choose a review path, bound the action, and preserve the residual.
